_patch_meta.description: Two Session B observation flags raised during VCB-Q01 re-evaluation: (1) G0570 apistia Registry 165 — 8 groups for 11 verses, 7 single-verse groups, warrants Session B consolidation assessment; (2) G4710 spoudē Registry 181 — 11 groups for 12 verses, 10 single-verse groups, Pauline earnestness groups may over-subdivide contextual variation.

record.description: G0570 apistia (mti_id=1198) has 8 active verse_context groups across 11 verses, with 7 groups containing only 1 verse each. The fine-grained subdivision from prior re-evaluation (vcb-010) creates many single-verse groups. While each group is analytically defensible at VC stage (1198-001 corporate covenantal; 1198-002 individual co-existing unbelief; 1198-003 apostolic-formation rebuke; 1198-004 rhetorical foil; 1198-005 negated role / Abraham; 1198-006 continuing-but-not-permanent; 1198-007 autobiographical pre-conversion; 1198-008 heart-susceptibility directive warning), Session B should assess whether the distinctions are operationally meaningful for the analytical programme or whether consolidation — e.g. into corporate covenantal / individual faith-struggle / rhetorical-negated / directive-warning groupings — better serves the characteristic-perspective model at analysis depth. The high group-to-verse ratio may create analytical fragmentation without proportionate gain.

operations.description: SBF-VCB-Q01-001: G0570 apistia — 8 groups / 11 verses; Session B consolidation assessment warranted

record.description: G4710 spoudē (mti_id=1268) has 11 active verse_context groups across 12 verses, with 10 groups containing only 1 verse each. The 2 physical-haste groups (1268-001 negative-moral urgency; 1268-002 positive-obedient eagerness) are clearly distinct. However the 9 earnestness/zeal groups (1268-003 through 1268-011) subdivide what may be a single inner-being characteristic — earnest disposition / zeal — across contextual variations (fruit of repentance, relational earnestness under discipline, listed virtue, evidence of love, God-given care, eschatological assurance, moral-formative effort, epistolary eagerness). Session B should assess whether these contextual variations reflect materially different inner-being characteristics or whether they subdivide the same characteristic by Pauline rhetorical occasion. Possible consolidation: physical haste (2 groups) / earnestness in body-life / earnestness as fruit or evidence of spiritual quality / earnestness as eschatological and formative orientation. The group descriptions are all well-formed at VC stage; the question is whether Session B analysis finds them analytically productive at this resolution.

operations.description: SBF-VCB-Q01-002: G4710 spoudē — 11 groups / 12 verses; Pauline earnestness over-subdivision concern for Session B
